masthead.title_html: Reason Your Way Through the World Cup, Vol. 2
masthead.title_plain: Reason Your Way Through the World Cup, Vol. 2
masthead.dek: A fresh round on football's biggest tournament — hosts and winners, players and records, and the odd corners of its history. Every answer routes to a country, a year, a count, or a famous name, reached by reasoning rather than rote. Narrow it down, place your bet, then tap to check.
masthead.footer_html: Built fresh against the existing World Cup set. Verified to FIFA, Britannica, RSSSF, and Guinness World Records.

plan.note: Three branches (history/hosts/winners, players/records, oddities), answers routing to countries, years/decades, counts, and famous players, each with a reflex-defeater.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · host-winners in the 21st century
questions.dimension: host-winners in the 21st century
questions.q: Six countries have lifted the men's World Cup while hosting it. Counting only the 21st century so far, how many of those host-winners are there?
questions.detail: France in 1998 was the last host nation to win; no host has won the trophy since.
questions.why: Players reach for a number, but the trick is that the streak of host triumphs stopped before 2000, so the count for this century is none.
questions.more: The full list of host-winners is Uruguay 1930, Italy 1934, England 1966, West Germany 1974, Argentina 1978, and France 1998.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first nation to host the tournament twice
questions.dimension: first nation to host the tournament twice
questions.q: One country was the first to stage the men's World Cup on two separate occasions, stepping in the second time after another nation pulled out. Which country?
questions.detail: Mexico hosted in 1970 and again in 1986, taking over after Colombia withdrew for economic reasons.
questions.why: Most early-host guesses go to Italy or France, but Mexico got the repeat first, and 2026 makes it the first three-time host.
questions.more: Colombia had been awarded 1986 but could not afford it, and Mexico was chosen as replacement in 1983.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first champion to win away from its home co
questions.dimension: first champion to win away from its home continent
questions.q: A champion winning on a continent other than its own sounds normal today, but it took decades. Which side was the first to win the World Cup outside its own continent?
questions.detail: Brazil won in Sweden in 1958, becoming the first nation to take the title on a different continent.
questions.why: Before 1958 every winner had triumphed at home or on its own landmass, so the South American breakthrough in Europe was the first.
questions.more: Brazil beat hosts Sweden 5-2, with Pele and Garrincha leading the side.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · host eliminated in the group stage with zer
questions.dimension: host eliminated in the group stage with zero points
questions.q: Only two host nations have ever crashed out in the opening group stage. South Africa was one. Which country was the other, going out without a single point?
questions.detail: Qatar lost all three group matches in 2022, the worst showing ever by a host.
questions.why: South Africa 2010 at least drew and won a game; Qatar is the host that lost everything, making it the pointless one.
questions.more: Qatar fell to Ecuador, Senegal, and the Netherlands, becoming the first host eliminated after just two matches.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · longest gap between tournaments
questions.dimension: longest gap between tournaments
questions.q: World wars meant some tournaments were simply never played. How many years separated the 1938 World Cup from the next one that actually took place?
questions.detail: After 1938 the next tournament was 1950, since both 1942 and 1946 were cancelled by the Second World War.
questions.why: The usual gap is four years, so reflex says four; the war wiped out two editions, stretching it to twelve.
questions.more: FIFA could not stage the event in 1942 or 1946 amid the war and its aftermath.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · deepest run by an Asian team
questions.dimension: deepest run by an Asian team
questions.q: Only one Asian side has ever reached the men's World Cup semifinals. Which country did it, finishing fourth as co-host?
questions.detail: South Korea reached the semifinals in 2002 and finished fourth, the best result by any Asian nation.
questions.why: Japan co-hosted the same year and is an easy wrong guess, but only South Korea went to the last four.
questions.more: South Korea beat Italy and Spain in the knockout rounds before losing to Germany and then to Turkey in the third-place game.
questions.source: Wikipedia South Korea at the FIFA World Cup (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Korea_at_the_FIFA_World_Cup)

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · total number of distinct champions
questions.dimension: total number of distinct champions
questions.q: Across all the men's World Cups ever held, how many different countries have actually won the trophy?
questions.detail: Eight nations have won: Brazil, Germany, Italy, Argentina, Uruguay, England, France, and Spain.
questions.why: Decades of tournaments make the count feel large, but the trophy has stayed in a remarkably small club of eight.
questions.more: Every one of those eight comes from either Europe or South America.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first team to reach three straight finals
questions.dimension: first team to reach three straight finals
questions.q: Reaching three World Cup finals in a row is rare. Which side was the first to do it, losing the first two before finally lifting the trophy in the third?
questions.detail: West Germany were runners-up in 1982 and 1986, then won in 1990.
questions.why: Brazil also did three straight finals later, but West Germany got there first, and the losses make it less obvious.
questions.more: Andreas Brehme's late penalty beat Argentina 1-0 in the 1990 final.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · decade of the first goalless final settled
questions.dimension: decade of the first goalless final settled on penalties
questions.q: Only one men's World Cup final has been decided by a penalty shootout with no goals scored in regular or extra time. In which decade was that final played?
questions.detail: The 1994 final between Brazil and Italy finished 0-0 and went to penalties, the first final ever decided that way.
questions.why: Later finals also reached penalties, but those had goals; the scoreless one belongs to the mid-1990s.
questions.more: Brazil won the shootout 3-2 after Roberto Baggio missed the decisive kick.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · continent of the first cross-continental co
questions.dimension: continent of the first cross-continental co-hosting
questions.q: The first World Cup staged outside both Europe and the Americas was also the first run by more than one country at once. On which continent was it held?
questions.detail: The 2002 tournament, co-hosted by South Korea and Japan, was the first in Asia and the first shared by two nations.
questions.why: People recall it as the Korea/Japan event but forget it was the debut both for Asia and for joint hosting.
questions.more: The final was played in Yokohama, Japan, where Brazil beat Germany.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · decade of the first back-to-back title defe
questions.dimension: decade of the first back-to-back title defense
questions.q: Only two nations have ever won the men's World Cup in back-to-back tournaments. Italy was the first. In which decade did Italy pull off that repeat?
questions.detail: Italy won in 1934 and again in 1938, the first country to retain the trophy.
questions.why: Brazil's repeat (1958-1962) often comes to mind first, but the original back-to-back champion did it in the 1930s.
questions.more: Italy and Brazil remain the only sides ever to win two in a row.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · size of the field at the first expansion fr
questions.dimension: size of the field at the first expansion from 16
questions.q: The men's World Cup spent decades with 16 finalists before growing. To how many teams did the field first expand in 1982?
questions.detail: The 1982 tournament in Spain was the first with 24 teams, up from 16.
questions.why: Many jump straight to the 32-team era, but the first step up was to 24, not 32.
questions.more: The expansion let more African and Asian sides qualify; Algeria, Cameroon, and others debuted.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · year of the last host-nation title
questions.dimension: year of the last host-nation title
questions.q: In which year did a host nation most recently win the men's World Cup on home soil?
questions.detail: France won as hosts in 1998; no host has won since.
questions.why: Hosts winning feels modern, but the last time it happened was the late 1990s, and it has not recurred.
questions.more: France beat Brazil 3-0 in the final in Paris.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · loser of the 2002 final
questions.dimension: loser of the 2002 final
questions.q: The 2002 men's World Cup final was an all-time heavyweight matchup. Which European side did Brazil beat to win that title in Asia?
questions.detail: Brazil beat Germany 2-0 in the 2002 final in Yokohama.
questions.why: People remember Brazil and Ronaldo but blank on the opponent, often guessing Italy or France.
questions.more: Ronaldo scored both goals; it was Germany's first World Cup final meeting with Brazil.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · small nation reaching a recent final
questions.dimension: small nation reaching a recent final
questions.q: Which country has reached two of the last three men's World Cup finals as a small nation, losing the 2018 final and then taking third in 2022?
questions.detail: Croatia lost the 2018 final to France and finished third in 2022.
questions.why: Their consistency hides behind bigger names, but no other small nation reached a final that recently.
questions.more: Croatia had also finished third in their 1998 debut.
questions.source: Wikipedia Croatia at the 2018 FIFA World Cup (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Croatia_at_the_2018_FIFA_World_Cup)

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · winner of the first-ever final
questions.dimension: winner of the first-ever final
questions.q: Which country won the very first World Cup final ever played, doing so in its own capital in 1930?
questions.detail: Uruguay beat Argentina 4-2 in Montevideo in the 1930 final.
questions.why: Brazil and Italy dominate early memory, but the trophy's very first holder was Uruguay.
questions.more: Uruguay is the smallest nation by population ever to win the men's title.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · gap between one nation's two titles
questions.dimension: gap between one nation's two titles
questions.q: After winning the first World Cup in 1930, Uruguay won it again. How many years passed between Uruguay's two titles?
questions.detail: Uruguay won in 1930 and again in 1950, twenty years apart.
questions.why: The two cancelled wartime tournaments stretch the gap far beyond the usual four-year rhythm.
questions.more: Their 1950 win came against hosts Brazil in the famous Maracana decider.
questions.source: Wikipedia Uruguay at the FIFA World Cup (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uruguay_at_the_FIFA_World_Cup)

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · year a European nation last won at home
questions.dimension: year a European nation last won at home
questions.q: Before 2026, when was the most recent men's World Cup that a European nation both hosted and won?
questions.detail: Italy won in 2006, but that tournament was in Germany; the last time a European host actually won was France 1998.
questions.why: This slicer separates 'European winner' from 'European host who won' and the right route is the late-1990s home win.
questions.more: European sides won 2006-2018, but only France 1998 did it on home soil among recent European champions.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · most recent first-time champion
questions.dimension: most recent first-time champion
questions.q: Among the countries that have won the men's World Cup, which one has lifted the trophy the fewest times while still being a multiple-time finalist that finally broke through only in the 2010s?
questions.detail: Spain won its first and only title in 2010, the newest name on the champions list.
questions.why: Long a heavyweight that never won, Spain is the latest country to join the winners' club.
questions.more: Spain beat the Netherlands 1-0 after extra time in the 2010 final.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · number of co-hosts in 2026
questions.dimension: number of co-hosts in 2026
questions.q: The 2026 men's World Cup will be split across more than one country, a setup used only a few times before. How many nations will jointly host in 2026?
questions.detail: The United States, Canada, and Mexico are co-hosting in 2026, the first time three nations share the tournament.
questions.why: Earlier joint hostings used two countries, so the leap to three is the surprising part.
questions.more: The expansion to 48 teams and 104 matches drove the decision to spread it across three nations.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · year of England's only final
questions.dimension: year of England's only final
questions.q: One former champion has reached the men's World Cup final exactly once in its entire history, winning that single appearance as host. In which year did that happen?
questions.detail: England reached its only World Cup final in 1966 and won it as host, beating West Germany 4-2.
questions.why: England is treated as a perennial contender, yet it has stood in only one final ever, the home win of 1966.
questions.more: England has not returned to a World Cup final since.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · decade a host lost its decisive home match
questions.dimension: decade a host lost its decisive home match
questions.q: A host nation losing its own final in front of a packed crowd is rare. In which decade did Brazil lose a deciding match as host, a result later nicknamed for the stadium?
questions.detail: Brazil lost the decisive 1950 match to Uruguay at the Maracana.
questions.why: It feels like ancient history, but it lands right at the turn into the 1950s, not earlier.
questions.more: The 1950 final round used a group format, and Uruguay's win is remembered as the Maracanazo.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · record number of semifinal appearances
questions.dimension: record number of semifinal appearances
questions.q: One country has reached the men's World Cup semifinals more often than any other, with no rival within two of its total. How many semifinals has it reached?
questions.detail: Germany has reached 13 World Cup semifinals, more than any other nation.
questions.why: Brazil feels like the natural answer, but Germany's tally of deep runs is the record, and no one else tops eleven.
questions.more: Germany reached the semis or better in 13 of its 20 appearances.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first European champion
questions.dimension: first European champion
questions.q: Which country won the World Cup as hosts in 1934, becoming the first European nation to win the trophy at all?
questions.detail: Italy won in 1934 on home soil, the first European winner after Uruguay's 1930 title.
questions.why: Germany and England feel like older powers, but Europe's first crown went to Italy.
questions.more: Italy beat Czechoslovakia 2-1 in the 1934 final.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · only hat-trick in a World Cup final
questions.dimension: only hat-trick in a World Cup final
questions.q: Many great strikers have hit two goals in a World Cup final, but only one man has ever scored three in a single final. Who?
questions.detail: Hurst scored three in England's 4-2 win over West Germany in the 1966 final at Wembley, and remains the only player with a final hat-trick.
questions.why: A reflex says Pele or a modern superstar, but none of them did it; Hurst's 1966 treble stood alone for 56 years until Mbappe matched it in a losing effort.
questions.more: Mbappe scored three in the 2022 final but France still lost on penalties, so Hurst is still the only one to do it on the winning side.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · won World Cup as both player and manager
questions.dimension: won World Cup as both player and manager
questions.q: Three men have won the World Cup both as a player on the pitch and later as the team's manager. Which Frenchman is the most recent to join that club?
questions.detail: Deschamps captained France to the 1998 title, then managed France to the 2018 trophy, joining Mario Zagallo and Franz Beckenbauer.
questions.why: The set is just three people, so a sharp fan can reason between Zagallo, Beckenbauer and the modern French boss who lifted it in 2018.
questions.more: Deschamps is also the only one of the three who did it as a winning captain and then as manager.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · most World Cup winner's medals by a player
questions.dimension: most World Cup winner's medals by a player
questions.q: Only one player has ever lifted the World Cup as a player three separate times. Who?
questions.detail: Pele was in Brazil's winning squads in 1958, 1962 and 1970, the only man with three winner's medals.
questions.why: Plenty of greats won it twice, but staying world-class across twelve years to win a third is the slicer; only one man managed it.
questions.more: He was also the youngest World Cup winner ever, at 17 in 1958.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · only player to score at four separate World
questions.dimension: only player to score at four separate World Cups
questions.q: The record for most career goals at the World Cup is held by a German who is also the only man to find the net at four different tournaments. Who is he?
questions.detail: Klose scored in 2002, 2006, 2010 and 2014, the only player to score at four tournaments, finishing as the all-time leading scorer.
questions.why: The 'four tournaments' angle narrows past one-tournament wonders like Fontaine and points to the long-serving German marksman.
questions.more: He passed Ronaldo's mark in the 2014 semi-final against Brazil, the 7-1 game.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · most goals at one World Cup by a single pla
questions.dimension: most goals at one World Cup by a single player
questions.q: One forward scored a barely believable thirteen goals at a single World Cup, in the only tournament he ever played. Name him.
questions.detail: France's Fontaine scored 13 goals at Sweden 1958, a single-tournament record that still stands, and never played another World Cup.
questions.why: The 'only tournament he ever played' twist points away from career stars to the one-off French phenomenon of 1958.
questions.more: The closest anyone has come since is Gerd Muller's 10 in 1970.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · nationality of the oldest World Cup goalsco
questions.dimension: nationality of the oldest World Cup goalscorer
questions.q: The oldest man ever to score a goal at a World Cup did it at age 42, with a bouncy corner-flag celebration. Which African country did he play for?
questions.detail: Roger Milla scored for Cameroon against Russia at USA 1994 aged 42 years and 39 days, the oldest scorer in tournament history.
questions.why: Routing to the nation keeps it walkable among World Cup countries, while the 42-year-old detail blocks a guess of a younger star.
questions.more: Milla had come out of retirement and also dazzled at Italy 1990.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first man to score at five separate World C
questions.dimension: first man to score at five separate World Cups
questions.q: Set aside every European and South American great. The first man ever to score in five different World Cups was a Portuguese forward. Name him.
questions.detail: Ronaldo scored in 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018 and 2022, becoming the first male player to score at five tournaments.
questions.why: Messi is the reflex, but it was Ronaldo who completed the five-tournament scoring run against Ghana in 2022.
questions.more: On the women's side, Marta reached five-tournament scoring first, in 2019.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · most World Cup matches by an outfield playe
questions.dimension: most World Cup matches by an outfield player
questions.q: The most matches any outfield player has played at World Cups is 25, set by a German who captained his country to the 1990 title. Who?
questions.detail: Matthaus played 25 World Cup matches across 1982-1998, a record, and captained West Germany to the 1990 trophy.
questions.why: The 'outfield' qualifier rules out the long-serving goalkeepers and the captaincy detail pins the famous German midfielder.
questions.more: He is one of only a handful of men to appear at five World Cups.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · scored in two separate World Cup finals
questions.dimension: scored in two separate World Cup finals
questions.q: Exactly five players have scored in two different World Cup finals. Which French No. 10 did it in 1998 and again in 2006?
questions.detail: Zidane scored twice in the 1998 final and once (a penalty) in the 2006 final, one of only five men to score in two finals.
questions.why: The two named years bracket a famous career and the small five-man set lets a fan reason to the French playmaker.
questions.more: His 2006 final ended with the infamous headbutt and a red card.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · count of players with a final hat-trick
questions.dimension: count of players with a final hat-trick
questions.q: How many different men have scored a hat-trick in the World Cup final across the tournament's whole history?
questions.detail: Only Geoff Hurst (1966) and Kylian Mbappe (2022) have scored three in a final.
questions.why: It feels like such a showcase that several would have done it, but the true count is just two across nearly a century.
questions.more: Mbappe's came in a losing cause, France falling to Argentina on penalties.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · uniqueness of Golden Boot winners
questions.dimension: uniqueness of Golden Boot winners
questions.q: No player has ever won the World Cup's top-scorer Golden Boot twice. So how many different men have won it across all tournaments since 1930?
questions.answer_subject: every tournament has had a different winner
questions.state: every tournament has had a different winner
questions.detail: No player has won the Golden Boot more than once, so each World Cup has produced a distinct top scorer.
questions.why: It seems likely a dominant striker repeated, but the surprising truth is that the award has never been successfully defended.
questions.more: Thomas Muller came closest, winning in 2010 and taking the Silver Boot in 2014.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · captain of a nation's lone World Cup title
questions.dimension: captain of a nation's lone World Cup title in 1986
questions.q: He captained his country to its only World Cup title in 1986 and won that tournament's best-player award too. Name him.
questions.detail: Maradona captained Argentina to the 1986 title and won the Golden Ball, dragging the side through with goals like those against England.
questions.why: Plain stem, but pairing captain-and-best-player in 1986 lands squarely on the Argentine maestro without naming him.
questions.more: His 'Hand of God' and solo goal both came in the 1986 quarter-final.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · nationality of the oldest World Cup player
questions.dimension: nationality of the oldest World Cup player
questions.q: The oldest man ever to appear in a World Cup match was a goalkeeper aged 45. Which African nation did he play for?
questions.detail: Essam El Hadary kept goal for Egypt against Saudi Arabia at the 2018 World Cup aged 45 years and 161 days, the oldest player ever.
questions.why: Routing to the country keeps a walkable set of World Cup nations while the keeper-at-45 detail blocks an outfield guess.
questions.more: He even saved a penalty in that match.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · most career goals in World Cup finals
questions.dimension: most career goals in World Cup finals
questions.q: How many goals did the player with the most World Cup final goals score across his two finals, a tally set by a Frenchman in 2018 and 2022?
questions.detail: Kylian Mbappe scored one in the 2018 final and a hat-trick in the 2022 final for four total, the most goals in finals by any player.
questions.why: Geoff Hurst's three is the reflex, but Mbappe's 1-plus-3 across two finals edges past everyone with four.
questions.more: He managed it despite France losing the 2022 final.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · nationality of the youngest World Cup playe
questions.dimension: nationality of the youngest World Cup player
questions.q: The youngest man ever to play in a World Cup match took the field at 17 years and 41 days. Which British home nation did he represent?
questions.detail: Norman Whiteside played for Northern Ireland against Yugoslavia at Spain 1982 aged 17 years and 41 days, younger even than Pele in 1958.
questions.why: Pele is the reflex for 'youngest', but he was older; the record belongs to a less obvious small-nation teenager.
questions.more: Whiteside beat Pele's mark by over 200 days.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · count of players reaching five World Cups
questions.dimension: count of players reaching five World Cups
questions.q: How many men have played at five separate World Cups, a club that includes Carbajal, Matthaus, Buffon, Marquez, Ronaldo and Messi?
questions.detail: Through 2022, six men had appeared at five World Cups: Carbajal, Matthaus, Buffon, Marquez, Ronaldo and Messi.
questions.why: The names are famous but the exact size of the club is the test, and several active stars push toward a sixth tournament in 2026.
questions.more: Messi, Ronaldo and Guillermo Ochoa were all in line to reach a sixth tournament at the 2026 World Cup.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · won as captain-player then as manager
questions.dimension: won as captain-player then as manager
questions.q: A German won the World Cup as a player captaining his side in 1974, then lifted it again as manager in 1990. Name him.
questions.detail: Beckenbauer captained West Germany to the 1974 title and managed them to the 1990 trophy, one of three to win as player and manager.
questions.why: Deschamps and Zagallo crowd the 'player and manager' idea; the 1974-captain plus 1990-manager pairing isolates the German libero.
questions.more: He is nicknamed Der Kaiser for his commanding sweeper play.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first World Cup to use a penalty shootout
questions.dimension: first World Cup to use a penalty shootout
questions.q: World Cup matches drawn after extra time are settled by a penalty shootout. Which World Cup was the first to ever use one to decide a match?
questions.detail: The first shootout in World Cup history came at Spain 1982, in the semifinal where West Germany beat France 5-4 after a 3-3 draw.
questions.why: Shootouts feel ancient, so people guess 1970 or earlier; in fact they only arrived in 1982 and many big tournaments before that replayed drawn knockout games.
questions.more: FIFA adopted the kicks-from-the-mark tiebreaker for World Cups starting that year; before it, drawn knockout matches were decided by replay or drawing lots.
questions.source: Britannica / Guinness World Records, 1982 FIFA World Cup penalty shootout history

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · nations in the only final decided on penalt
questions.dimension: nations in the only final decided on penalties
questions.q: Only once has the World Cup final itself been decided by a penalty shootout after a scoreless 120 minutes. Which two nations were on the pitch that night?
questions.detail: At the 1994 final in Pasadena, Brazil beat Italy 3-2 on penalties after 0-0; Roberto Baggio skied the decisive kick.
questions.why: The 2006 and 2022 finals also went to penalties, but both had goals first; only 1994 was a goalless final settled purely from the spot.
questions.more: It earned Brazil a fourth title and is remembered for Baggio's miss over the bar.
questions.source: Guinness World Records, First penalty shoot-out in a FIFA World Cup final

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · host that produced the first mascot
questions.dimension: host that produced the first mascot
questions.q: The very first official World Cup mascot, introduced in 1966, was a cartoon lion in a football kit. Which host nation produced him?
questions.detail: World Cup Willie, a lion in a Union Flag jersey, debuted for England 1966 and launched modern tournament merchandising.
questions.why: Mascots feel like a recent gimmick, so 1966 surprises people; and a lion points to England rather than a warmer-climate host.
questions.more: Willie was drawn by illustrator Reg Hoye and even had his own theme song.
questions.source: Britannica / List of FIFA World Cup official mascots; FourFourTwo

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · team count at the inaugural tournament
questions.dimension: team count at the inaugural tournament
questions.q: The very first World Cup, in 1930, had no qualifying rounds at all - teams simply accepted invitations. How many nations took part?
questions.detail: Thirteen nations entered the 1930 tournament by invitation, the only World Cup ever held without any qualifying.
questions.why: People expect a round number like 16; the reflex misses that the first edition was a small invitational of 13.
questions.more: Only four European sides made the long boat journey: Belgium, France, Romania and Yugoslavia.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · the only World Cup with no final match
questions.dimension: the only World Cup with no final match
questions.q: Exactly one World Cup never staged a single championship final match - the title was instead decided by a four-team round-robin. Which tournament was it?
questions.detail: Brazil 1950 used a final group stage rather than a one-off final; the decisive game just happened to be the last fixture.
questions.why: Everyone assumes every World Cup ends in a final, so the one that technically didn't is a genuine trap - and it is not the inaugural 1930 edition.
questions.more: FIFA returned to a single championship final in 1954 and never repeated the format.
questions.source: Wikipedia, Uruguay v Brazil (1950 FIFA World Cup); footballhistory.org

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · the only previously co-hosted tournament
questions.dimension: the only previously co-hosted tournament
questions.q: Before 2026, only one World Cup had ever been shared between two host nations. Which tournament was co-hosted?
questions.detail: The 2002 World Cup was split between South Korea and Japan, the first and only joint hosting before the three-country 2026 edition.
questions.why: Co-hosting feels common now, but it had happened exactly once before; people often misremember an earlier example.
questions.more: It was also the first World Cup held in Asia.
questions.source: Britannica / footballhistory.org, 2002 FIFA World Cup Korea/Japan

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · count of hosts that won at home
questions.dimension: count of hosts that won at home
questions.q: Counting every edition through 2022, how many different host nations have gone on to win the World Cup on their own soil?
questions.detail: Uruguay 1930, Italy 1934, England 1966, West Germany 1974, Argentina 1978 and France 1998 all won as hosts.
questions.why: Home advantage feels decisive, so people guess higher; in nearly a century it has happened just six times, and not since 1998.
questions.more: Brazil, despite hosting twice, is conspicuously absent from this list.
questions.source: Opta Analyst / SI, World Cup host nations performance

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · team count of the expanded 2026 field
questions.dimension: team count of the expanded 2026 field
questions.q: For 2026 the World Cup grew to its largest field ever. How many teams now play in the finals?
questions.detail: The 2026 tournament expanded to 48 teams, up from the 32-team format used since 1998.
questions.why: People who know the field grew may overshoot to 64 or undershoot to 40; the agreed figure is 48.
questions.more: The jump also created an unprecedented 104-match schedule.

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · city of the three-time World Cup stadium
questions.dimension: city of the three-time World Cup stadium
questions.q: One famous old stadium will, in 2026, become the first ground ever to host matches at three different World Cups. In which city does it stand?
questions.detail: Estadio Azteca in Mexico City staged matches in 1970 and 1986 and will again in 2026 - a first for any stadium.
questions.why: Several stadiums hosted two World Cups, so naming the unique three-time one requires recalling the 1970/1986/2026 thread, not just a famous venue.
questions.more: It is also the only stadium to have hosted two World Cup finals.
questions.source: FIFA / Al Jazeera, Estadio Azteca to host 2026 opening match

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · first nation to take Oceania's automatic be
questions.dimension: first nation to take Oceania's automatic berth
questions.q: Oceania's confederation existed for decades without ever getting a guaranteed World Cup spot of its own. Which 2026 nation became the first to claim that brand-new automatic berth?
questions.detail: With the 48-team expansion, Oceania got a direct berth for the first time, and New Zealand seized it in qualifying.
questions.why: People might guess Australia, but Australia long ago left Oceania for Asia; the berth fell to New Zealand.
questions.more: Before 2026, Oceania teams always had to survive an intercontinental play-off.
questions.source: NBC Sports / FIFA, Oceania 2026 World Cup qualifying

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · smallest qualifier by population
questions.dimension: smallest qualifier by population
questions.q: By population, the smallest nation ever to qualify for a World Cup did so for 2026, with only about 158,000 people. Which country is it?
questions.detail: Curacao, a Caribbean island of roughly 158,000, qualified for 2026, breaking Iceland's previous record.
questions.why: Iceland (about 350,000) is the famous tiny qualifier, so it is the natural wrong answer; Curacao is less than half its size.
questions.more: Iceland had held the smallest-nation record since 2018.
questions.source: World Soccer Talk / Yahoo Sports, smallest 2026 World Cup nations

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · Central Asian World Cup debutant for 2026
questions.dimension: Central Asian World Cup debutant for 2026
questions.q: Four nations will make their World Cup debut in 2026. One is a Central Asian country appearing for the very first time. Which one?
questions.detail: Uzbekistan debuts in 2026 alongside Cape Verde, Curacao and Jordan.
questions.why: Several Central Asian sides have come close before, so picking the right first-timer requires the specific 2026 debutant list.
questions.more: It is the first Central Asian nation to reach the men's World Cup.
questions.source: Al Jazeera, which teams debut at World Cup 2026

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · tournament with the most shootouts
questions.dimension: tournament with the most shootouts
questions.q: The most penalty shootouts ever crammed into a single World Cup happened in one recent tournament, which ended with a shootout in the final itself. Which tournament set that record?
questions.detail: Qatar 2022 produced five shootouts, capped by Argentina beating France on penalties in the final.
questions.why: The drama of 1990 or 2006 makes them tempting, but the record total of five belongs to 2022.
questions.more: The previous high had been four shootouts in a tournament.
questions.source: Guinness World Records, most penalty shoot-outs at a FIFA World Cup

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · host country of the 2026 final
questions.dimension: host country of the 2026 final
questions.q: The 2026 World Cup final will be played just outside New York City. Which one of the three host countries gets the final?
questions.detail: The final is set for MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, in the United States, on July 19, 2026.
questions.why: With Canada and Mexico also hosting, and Mexico holding the famous opener, people may misattribute the final's location.
questions.more: FIFA refers to the venue as 'New York New Jersey Stadium' for sponsorship reasons.
questions.source: FIFA / Wikipedia, 2026 FIFA World Cup final

questions.cat: FIFA World Cup · animal used as the 1994 mascot
questions.dimension: animal used as the 1994 mascot
questions.q: The 1994 World Cup mascot broke a long run of cartoon characters by being an animal in a national kit. Which animal was it?
questions.detail: Striker, an anthropomorphic dog in a red-white-and-blue 'USA 94' kit, was designed by Warner Bros.
questions.why: People remember the orange (1982) or the lion (1966) and may guess an eagle for the U.S.; the actual 1994 mascot was a dog.
questions.more: Striker brought an animal back as a mascot for the first time since the 1966 lion.
questions.source: SI / FIFA, 1994 World Cup mascot Striker
